targets.text:
Question: A three-phase synchronous generator is to be connected to the infinite bus. The lamps are connected as shown in the for the synchronization. The phase sequence of bus voltage is R-Y-B and that of incoming generator voltage is R'-Y'-B'.

It was found that the lamps are becoming dark in the sequence \( L_a - L_b - L_c \). It means that the phase sequence of the incoming generator is ______.

Choices:
A. opposite to infinite bus and its frequency is more than infinite bus 
B. opposite to infinite bus but its frequency is less than infinite bus 
C. same as infinite bus and its frequency is more than infinite bus 
D. same as infinite bus and its frequency is less than infinite bus

answer_slots.target_text: It means that the phase sequence of the incoming generator is ______.
answer_slots.expected_answer: opposite to infinite bus and its frequency is more than infinite bus

sentence_segments.text: Question: A three-phase synchronous generator is to be connected to the infinite bus.
The lamps are connected as shown in the for the synchronization.
The phase sequence of bus voltage is R-Y-B and that of incoming generator voltage is R'-Y'-B'.
It was found that the lamps are becoming dark in the sequence \( L_a - L_b - L_c \).
It means that the phase sequence of the incoming generator is ______.
opposite to infinite bus and its frequency is more than infinite bus
opposite to infinite bus but its frequency is less than infinite bus
same as infinite bus and its frequency is more than infinite bus
same as infinite bus and its frequency is less than infinite bus

conditions.text: Question: A thin soap bubble of radius \( R = 1 \) cm, and thickness \( a = 3.3 \mu m \ (a << R) \), is at a potential of 1V with respect to a reference point at infinity.
The volume of the soap in the thin bubble is \( 4\pi R^2 a \) and that of the drop is \( \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 \).

targets.text:
Question: A thin soap bubble of radius \( R = 1 \) cm, and thickness \( a = 3.3 \mu m \ (a << R) \), is at a potential of 1 V with respect to a reference point at infinity. The bubble bursts and becomes a single spherical drop of soap (assuming all the soap is contained in the drop) of radius \( r \). The volume of the soap in the thin bubble is \( 4\pi R^2 a \) and that of the drop is \( \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 \). The potential in volts, of the resulting single spherical drop with respect to the same reference point at infinity is ______. (Give the answer up to two decimal places.)

Choices: 
A. 10.03 
B. 20.25 
C. 30.28 
D. 40.08

answer_slots.target_text: (Give the answer up to two decimal places.)

sentence_segments.text: Question: A thin soap bubble of radius \( R = 1 \) cm, and thickness \( a = 3.3 \mu m \ (a << R) \), is at a potential of 1V with respect to a reference point at infinity.
The bubble bursts and becomes a single spherical drop of soap (assuming all the soap is contained in the drop) of radius \( r \).
The volume of the soap in the thin bubble is \( 4\pi R^2 a \) and that of the drop is \( \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 \).
The potential in V, of the resulting single spherical drop with respect to the same reference point at infinity is ______.
(Give the answer up to two decimal places.)

conditions.text: Question: It is required to design an anti-aliasing filter for an 8-bit ADC.
The filter is a first-order RC filter with \( R = 1\Omega \) and \( C = 1F \).

targets.text: What is the SNR (in dB) of the ADC?
Also, find the frequency (in decades) at which the filter attenuation just exceeds the SNR of the ADC.

answer_slots.target_text: Also, find the frequency (in decades) at which the filter attenuation just exceeds the SNR of the ADC.

sentence_segments.text: Question: It is required to design an anti-aliasing filter for an 8-bit ADC.
The filter is a first-order RC filter with \( R = 1\Omega \) and \( C = 1F \).
The ADC is designed to span a sinusoidal signal with peak-to-peak amplitude equal to the full-scale range of the ADC.
What is the SNR (in dB) of the ADC?
Also, find the frequency (in decades) at which the filter attenuation just exceeds the SNR of the ADC.
